SKILLS
If two characters attempt t0 use Quickdraw on each other; make an Opposed Test: The character with the higher Margin of Success gets his attack off first: Note that Quickdraw can only be used at the beginning of combat encounter and is surprise move The gamemaster should   determine  modifiers   according to the enemy' 5 alerte ness knowledge of the skilled character' concealed weapon ad whether the enemy already has drawn weapons aimed at the character attempting this move
in tne immediate area but does not rule out  offpianet ship ments black-market contacts_ Note that the Scrounge Skill does not help the character purchase the commodity at good price, Once he locates the item sought; the character would use the Negotiation Skill for haggling:
SECURITY SYSTEMS The Security Systems Skill confers familiarity with security measures such as locks alarms remote cameras _ motion sen- sors and So On Characters must make Security Systems Skill Checks when attempting to defeat such security systems_ When character has puL new security system into place_ his Security Systems Skill Bonus is added to any interloper' = target number to defeat the system. For attempts to defeat locks with Security System Action Checks the gamemaster should first assign difficulty ratings to all locks_ lock' s rating is used t0 modify the target number of any characters attempting to defeat the lock. Simple archaic locks should be easy (-21, while advanced locks should have positive ratings Defeating some advanced locks_ most notably electronic code locks may require special equipment. Such equipment can be found electronic  security bypass kits (Equipment; 1291, obtained through tne use the   Scrounge Streetwise Skills. Attempting to open complicated electronic lock with no equipment at all is futile; the character might as well destroy the door:
RIDING The Riding Skill confers familiarity with all forms f natural land transportation from horses t0 more exotic riding animals The skill also confers the ability tO drive carriages and wagons drawn by beasts Characters make Riding Action   Checks wnenever iney attempt difficult or dangerous maneuvers while riding an anima operating carriage or wagon The gamemaster sets roll modifiers based on the type of maneuver attempted, the crea ture' temperament and the character' familiarity with the ani" mal. At the gamemaster discretion_ failed roll may result in the beast simply continuing to move forward or even throwing Its rider; Chases using riding beasts can be resolved in the same #ay as vehicle chases (see Piloting, p. 104), using Riding Skill in the place of Piloting Skill:
RifLES This weapon skili is used to make attacks with all rifle and rifle-like weapons, including assault rifles sniper rifles and laser niles:
SEDUCTION character with the Seduction Skill can effectively use wit _ guile; looks and pure personal magnetism to charm another character: Note that this skill can be used on individuals of any gender  because docs not necessarily involve lusttul advances Rather, the Seduction Skill involves careful appli- cation of compliments and ego boosting that lowers the target' s guard and brings him into the seducing character' confidence Characters make Seduction Action Checks to determine if their attempts seduce target are successful. The gamemaster modifies the target number based on the target'$ feelings toward the character and character'$ general approach; which should be established through roleplaying: The may also be modified based on the information the character seeks from the target or the actions he wants the target t0 perform. Aside from acquiring information; Seduction can also used t0 gain trust; favors or preferential treatment in certain sit- uations_ It can also provide helpful foundation for the use of other SOC-related skills such as Fast Talk or Negotiation_ The use of such skills on previously seduced target would receive modifier of -1 3 , depending on how effective the seduc tion was
RUNNING The Running Skill enables characters to run faster and far- ther than characters without the skill (see Movement; 110). Chases on foot can be resolved in the same way as vehi- cle chases see Piloting: p. 1041, using Running Skill in the place of Piloting Skill.
BCROUNOE character with the Scrounge Skill has knack for acquir- ing materials that are  normally difficult to obtain:  This Srili assumes the character has network contacts able to help locate whatever he requires and that he is adept at uncov- erng existing networks establishing new ones wherever he goes. Locating desired commodity or service often requires an exchange of favors or other materials and becomes kind of chain reaction throughout the network: Locating desired commodity requires Skill Check: Assign modifiers based on the value ad rarity of the desired commodity; the time the scavenger has for his work and how well-established his network in the area Eventually. scavenger will be able to find what he is look- ing for unless the gamemaster decides not available_ failed Action Check means that the commodity is not available
